description: Add an LlmRunner trait abstraction with ClaudeRunner + GrokRunner impls, plus a 5th rung in the overflow recovery ladder and a RuntimeError fallback hook that promote tasks to Grok when the Claude ladder is exhausted. Default disabled; opt-in via .task-mgr/config.json. Phase 1 is a zero-behavior-change trait extraction (foundation); Phase 2 lights up Grok routing for explicit task models; Phase 3 wires the policy. Architect-reviewed and revised to handle 10+ edge cases around override precedence, tasks.model DB column writes, wave-mode wiring, idempotency guards, auth-failure short-circuits, and operator escape valves.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: Scoped tests for touched files pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing public spawn_claude signature (preserved via type aliases SpawnOpts = RunnerOpts and ClaudeResult = RunnerResult)
No new .unwrap() or .expect() in production code paths (existing #[cfg(test)] allow continues)
All eprintln! warnings include enough context to identify the failed operation (task ID, operation name, path if applicable)
Behavior unchanged when fallbackRunner config is absent or enabled:false — byte-identical to today's 4-rung ladder ending in Blocked
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding (operator escape valve, override-shadowing, wave-mode wiring)
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — runner trait extraction now pays back 10x when adding OpenAI/Gemini later
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — pragmatic, reliable, wired in per PRD
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Single source of truth for effective_runner — compute once per iteration, pass to both spawn and idempotency guards
effective_model passed to handle_prompt_too_long MUST be the post-override value
Phase 1 must be byte-for-byte parity — type aliases preserve all 10 spawn_claude call sites
Wave + sequential mode share handle_prompt_too_long — overflow rung 4 works in both with no path-specific code
Wave-mode RuntimeError hook runs in post-wave aggregation on the main thread, NEVER in slot workers (IterationContext is not thread-safe)
tasks.model DB UPDATE is contractual when fallback fires — otherwise resolve_task_model shadows the in-memory override on next iteration
Operator intent wins — explicit tasks.model edit mid-loop clears all overrides on next iteration
In-memory overrides only — restart drops state (matches existing pattern), at most one wasted Claude iteration after restart per affected task
Token-equality provider detection — substring matching incorrectly classifies 'groq-llama-3' (Groq Inc.) as xAI Grok

prohibitedOutcomes: Substring matching .contains('grok') for provider detection — must use token-equality on '-' splits to avoid mis-routing 'groq-llama' (Groq Inc.) to xAI Grok
Re-deriving effective_runner in multiple places — compute ONCE per iteration and pass the value through
Passing prompt_result.resolved_model (pre-override) to handle_prompt_too_long — must pass effective_model (post-override) or idempotency guard breaks silently
Box<dyn LlmRunner> dynamic dispatch — use enum RunnerKind + static match dispatch (allocation-free; exhaustive-match guards against missing variant handlers)
OR-style idempotency guard (runner_overrides.get(task) OR provider_for_model(model)) — pin to the single computed effective_runner value; OR masks future drift
Skipping the tasks.model DB UPDATE when overflow rung 4 or RuntimeError hook sets runner_overrides — without it, resolve_task_model on next iteration silently shadows the override
Running RuntimeError fallback hook inside a slot worker — IterationContext is not thread-safe; wire it into the post-wave aggregation step on the main thread
Persisting runner_overrides / model_overrides / effort_overrides to DB — design decision is in-memory only (matches existing pattern); restart clears state
Counting TaskMgrError::GrokAuthFailure toward consecutive_failures — auth lapses must not push a healthy task into auto_block_task with a misleading reason
Parsing 'grok --help' output to check flag availability — brittle; minimum version goes in user-facing docs only
Tests that hand-build HashMaps to mock IterationContext state — use the real struct so wrong field names fail at compile time, not silently
Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Migrating background spawn_claude callers (curate, learnings, milestone, prd_reconcile, watchdog) to direct LlmRunner — out of v1 scope; they keep using the spawn_claude wrapper

userStories.description: FR-005 modifies engine.rs::escalate_task_model_if_needed (lines ~4581-4609): today it tops out at Opus; this PRD adds a Grok pivot when fallback is enabled AND tasks.consecutive_failures >= threshold AND model is already Opus. This task identifies all consumers of the function and the tasks.model column it writes, documents semantic distinctions between 'Claude escalation' and 'Grok promotion' contexts, and confirms whether the change is additive (gated on config) or modifies existing behavior under default settings.
userStories.notes:
Per PRD §6 Consumers of Changed Behavior: engine.rs:4599-4602 writes tasks.model on Opus escalation. The fallback hook must also UPDATE tasks.model to grok-model in the same logical operation — otherwise on the next iteration resolve_task_model reads task_row.model = OPUS_MODEL (highest precedence at model.rs:150-152) and the in-memory runner override is silently shadowed. This analysis pins the contract.

Reference: PRD §6 'Consumers of Changed Behavior' table, §2.5 'tasks.model DB column interaction' Correctness Requirement.

No code changes — output is a progress-file Consumer Impact Table.
qualityDimensions: Read actual code; never infer call sites from variable names

Distinguish 'additive under default config' from 'modifies existing behavior' — the architect's review found the change is additive (gated) but the tasks.model write interaction with resolve_task_model is contractual and could regress silently if mishandled
Output is a Consumer Impact Table in the progress file with concrete file:line citations

userStories.description: Phase 3, US-006 + FR-008. Implement the override-invalidation check that runs before every iteration's runner selection. The check reads tasks.model from DB and compares to overflow_original_task_model[task_id]. If they differ (operator edited the explicit model), drop runner_overrides, model_overrides, effort_overrides for that task AND remove from overflow_recovered AND remove overflow_original_model AND remove overflow_original_task_model. Emit a one-line stderr notice. This gives the operator a mid-loop escape valve.
userStories.notes:
Reference: PRD §3 US-006 ACs; PRD §6 FR-008; PRD §2.5 'Override-clearing on explicit task-model change' (Correctness Requirement).

This is the operator escape valve. Without it, auto-recovery decisions stick until loop restart — bad UX when the operator decides to take manual control of a stuck task mid-loop.

Detection precondition: the operator changed `tasks.model` via `task-mgr loop init --append --update-existing` OR via direct DB edit. Both paths land in the same place (tasks.model column).

The six fields to clear (per PRD §2.5 'Override-clearing'):
1. runner_overrides[task_id]
2. model_overrides[task_id]
3. effort_overrides[task_id]
4. overflow_recovered.remove(task_id)
5. overflow_original_model[task_id]
6. overflow_original_task_model[task_id]

Place the call at the TOP of each iteration's task-processing block (engine.rs:561 for wave, engine.rs:2285 for sequential), BEFORE effective_runner is computed.
qualityDimensions: Reads tasks.model FRESH from DB on each iteration (NOT cached) — operator edits land immediately
Six-field clear is atomic from the caller's perspective — wrapped in one function, no partial clears
Stderr notice is informative and identifies the task ID
No-op when there's nothing to invalidate (task_id absent from overflow_original_task_model) — cheap to call unconditionally
userStories.title: Override invalidation check on every iteration start (operator escape valve)

userStories.description:
From /review-loop on 2026-05-18 (H2, H3, M1, M2, M3, M5, L1-L5 bundled into one atomic fix). Implement each finding below; run cargo test --lib loop_engine + cargo clippy after every batch; commit at the end of the task.

## H2 — was_at_opus misses Opus[1M] (src/loop_engine/engine.rs:4913)
Current: `let was_at_opus = matches!(current_model.as_deref(), Some(m) if m == model::OPUS_MODEL);` — string-eq excludes `claude-opus-4-7[1m]`. Goal #2 of the PRD ('after the Opus ceiling is reached') intends the Opus *tier*, not the exact ID. Fix: use the inclusive ModelTier check (e.g. `matches!(model::model_tier(current_model.as_deref()), ModelTier::Opus)` — confirm the exact helper name in src/loop_engine/model.rs). Add a regression test in tests/runtime_error_fallback.rs that puts a task on `claude-opus-4-7[1m]` with consecutive_failures >= threshold and asserts promotion to Grok.

## M1 — u32 → i32 cast (engine.rs:4914)
Same line region. `new_count < fallback.runtime_error_threshold as i32` casts u32 lossily at >= 2^31. Compare in u32 space (or guard the cast). Default threshold is 2, so no behavior change for sane inputs; cleanup only.

## H3 — effective_runner re-derivation drift guard
resolve_effective_runner is called at engine.rs:1456, 1877, 2437, 2731, 4917. PRD §2.5 mandates a single computed value per iteration to prevent silent drift. Choose the lighter fix: at each non-primary call site, add `debug_assert_eq!(resolve_effective_runner(...), <primary_computed_value>);` cross-checks where the primary value is in scope. If threading the single value is cleaner, do that instead — but keep the diff small. Goal: a future refactor that returns the wrong value at one site fails a debug build before reaching production.

## M2 — Double eprintln! on promotion
Find the two promotion eprintln! sites (rung-4 in overflow.rs and the RuntimeError hook in engine.rs ~4937). Under wave-mode aggregation a single task can hit both in one wave. Consolidate so each promotion emits exactly one banner line per iteration. Acceptable approach: gate the second eprintln! on the override map being empty (i.e. first-time promotion only), or move the banner to a shared site after both gates have run.

## M3 — check_fallback_runner_binary does not honor $GROK_BINARY (project_config.rs:283)
The startup check resolves `cli_binary or 'grok'` but skips the env var, while GrokRunner::spawn uses `$GROK_BINARY → cli_binary → 'grok'`. Mismatch confuses operators who set GROK_BINARY for test/dev. Update check_fallback_runner_binary to use the same resolution chain. Update tests/fallback_config.rs to cover the env-var-set case.

## M5 — Fixed 3s GROK_AUTH_FAILURE_WINDOW with no env override (runner.rs:41)
Add TASK_MGR_GROK_AUTH_WINDOW_SECS env override mirroring the CLAUDE_BINARY test-only pattern. Parse u64, fall back to 3 on missing/invalid. Document in rustdoc as a test-only seam. Update tests/grok_auth_failure.rs to use the env override for the fast/slow case rather than depending on real timing.

## L1 — Temp-script test cleanup hardening
tests/grok_auth_failure.rs and tests/grok_runner_unit.rs use std::env::temp_dir() with `let _ = std::fs::remove_file(...)` cleanup. Wrap in a tempfile::TempDir or a Drop guard so test panics don't leak files.

## L2 — Rename grok_model → target_model in RecoveryAction::FallbackToProvider.user_message (overflow.rs:77-81)
The provider name is configurable; the local should not bake 'grok' into the identifier. Pure rename.

## L3 — Disambiguate FEAT-007 in IterationContext::crashed_last_iteration rustdoc (engine.rs:235-238)
The doc references 'FEAT-007' but this PRD also has its own FEAT-007. Clarify which PRD/scope.

## L4 — Comment substring-vs-token divergence in model.rs
model::model_tier uses substring matching while model::provider_for_model uses token equality (intentional). A short comment near either function pinning the divergence prevents a future 'fix' that aligns them and breaks Groq Inc. routing.

## L5 — Reorder six-field clear rustdoc in engine.rs:4812-4817 to match code order
Cosmetic: the rustdoc lists the cleared fields in one order and the code clears them in another. Align.

## Out of scope here
L6 (the diff-stat 'deletions' that resolve when H1 merges main) is resolved by 5ba153a7-REVIEW-FIX-1-MERGE-MAIN. M4 was already resolved during review (assertion exists at tests/grok_runner_unit.rs:167).
